title: Radio Silence on Abigail and Scream Departure
text: Directors Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett discussed their transition from the Scream franchise to their vampire film Abigail in an interview with The Hollywood Reporter. After the success of Scream VI, which set domestic box office records for the franchise, Radio Silence wanted to pursue other projects. We talk a lot about wanting to model our careers after filmmakers like Ron Howard and Rob Reiner, Gillett explained, noting they desired creative variety before potentially returning to Ghostface. However, Spyglass Media preferred to maintain momentum rather than pause production. A month after the premiere of Scream VI, we had this go-project at Universal that we had really fallen in love with, Gillett said, referencing Abigail. The directors acknowledged the chaos that followed their departure, including Jenna Ortega and Melissa Barrera's exits from Scream VII. It's just a challenge when you see people that you care about dealing with any form of challenge, Gillett reflected. Despite the turmoil, they remained focused on Abigail's demanding production schedule. Radio Silence made clear they intend to work with Barrera again: We're always excited and going to be excited and looking to work with Melissa again, Gillett stated.
